steps.m: A-L-M (three special letters at the start of this chapter)
steps.n: These are three special letters that appear at the start of some chapters in the Quran. We say each letter's full name: 'alif' (A), 'laam' (L), 'meem' (M). While scholars have discussed many possible meanings, Allah knows their true meaning best.
This is the basic part that means 'that' for feminine things
Adding ل with sukun helps specify this is for pointing to things

steps.m: that (pointing to something far away, feminine) (describes ءَايٰتُ)
steps.n: Adding كَ shows we're pointing to something far away. This word describes ءَايٰتُ (signs/verses) in this ayah.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be evident

steps.n: The fatha vowel helps form the singular noun 'sign'
This is already plural - both words mean multiple signs. The main difference is just in how it's written
Added ُ to show this word is the subject of تِلْكَ (those) in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he wrote' into 'what was written' by using a special pattern that makes nouns from actions
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific book
The word changed to show it belongs to/describes something else (the verses)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
This pattern shows someone or something has a lot of this quality
Adding ال makes it refer to something specific

steps.m: of the wise one (describing ٱلْكِتٰبِ - the book)
steps.n: The kasra shows this word describes 'the book' (ٱلْكِتٰبِ) in the phrase 'verses of the wise book'

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Changed from a verb root meaning 'to guide' into a noun meaning 'guidance' by adding the vowel marks
Added tanween and changed final ي to ى to show this is an indefinite abstract concept
Adding vowels to create the basic noun meaning 'mercy'

steps.m: a specific act of kindness and care
steps.n: Added ة to make it refer to one specific instance

steps.m: and a specific act of kindness and care
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect it with هُدًى (guidance) that comes before it

steps.m: and as a mercy (connected to هُدًى/guidance)
steps.n: Added ً to show this word follows the same grammar as هُدًى (guidance) before it
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start to make it mean 'someone who' and changed the middle of the word to show they are doing good deeds
Added ين to make it plural (more than two)
Added لِ at beginning to show 'for/to'

steps.m: for those who do good (guidance and mercy for them)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل for emphasis and َ at end to complete the word. This word shows who receives the guidance and mercy mentioned before it (هُدًى وَرَحْمَةً).
Added ن to make it refer to multiple people
Added ال with shadda to point to specific people. This word describes the people who do the actions in the rest of the verse (establish prayer, give charity, and are certain of the Hereafter).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The middle weak letter و changes to ا due to Arabic phonetic rules
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowel to ي (makes it mean 'establishes' instead of 'stood')
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word describes 'those who' (الَّذِينَ) at the start of the ayah.

steps.m: to perform ritual prayer, to connect with Allah

steps.n: Basic vowel marks (fatha) added above letters to make the root readable as 'salawa'
Added ة to make it a specific instance of prayer
Added 'the' (ال) at the start, which joins with ص to make it stronger
Added overbar (alif khanjariyah) over و as standard spelling

steps.m: the ritual prayer (object of يُقِيمُونَ - they establish)
steps.n: Added fatha to show this is the object of the verb يُقِيمُونَ (they establish)

steps.m: gives/brings forth (happening now or in future)
steps.n: Added يُ at the beginning to show the action is happening now or will happen. The ؤ helps connect the sounds smoothly.
Added ون at the end to show multiple people (the believers) are doing this action

steps.m: and they give/bring forth (connected to يُقِيمُونَ - they establish)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this with the previous action (establishing prayer)

steps.m: to purify or make something grow

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the word to show the act of purifying itself (like changing 'purify' to 'purification'). The ة at the end makes it a specific instance.
Added ال to show we're talking about a specific charity. The ل combines with ز to make زّ (with shadda)

steps.m: the obligatory charity (that they give يُؤْتُونَ)
steps.n: Added َ at the end to show this is what's being given. The alif changed to وٰ because it's a special spelling in the Quran.
Added damma to show this is a complete pronoun meaning 'they'
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement about those who establish prayer and give zakah

steps.m: to come after, to be last

steps.n: Adding ة changes it from 'last' to mean 'the last life' or 'afterlife'
Adds ال to make it refer to THE afterlife specifically
Adds بِ to show this is what they believe IN (connected to يُوقِنُونَ - they are certain)
The letter ِ (kasra) is added at the end because it comes after the word بِ (bi)

This is a pronoun that refers to a group of people. In this ayah, it's referring to the same people mentioned before (those who establish prayer and give zakah) and is the subject of the verb يُوقِنُونَ (they are certain).

steps.m: to be completely sure about something

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and و after it. This shows the action is happening now.

steps.m: they are completely sure (referring to those who establish prayer and give zakah)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing this action. This refers back to 'those who' (الَّذِينَ) mentioned at the start of the verse.
This is the base form of the demonstrative pronoun used to point to things
The alif with line (ٰ) extends the sound to indicate distance
Adding ئِ makes it more specific, like saying 'those specific ones'

steps.m: those ones there (far away) (subject of هُمُ)
steps.n: The كَ at the end completes the demonstrative pronoun. This word is the subject of هُمُ (they are) in this ayah.
This is the basic form showing the concept of being high or elevated
Adding ى changes this from a concept into a preposition that shows one thing is on top of or based on another. Here it shows the previous people mentioned are upon guidance.
The small alif (ٰ) is added as a pronunciation helper to show the long 'aa' sound at the end
Changes from verb 'to guide' into the noun 'guidance'
Changed final ي to ى and added tanween (ً) to show this is complete guidance that can benefit others
This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts
When this word comes after a word ending in tanween (like هُدًى), the ن sound combines with the م, making it stronger with a shadda mark

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop something to its full potential

steps.n: The root 'r-b-b' becomes 'rabb' (lord), then the kasra ending shows it's being used to say 'my lord'

steps.m: their Lord (referring to those who are guided in مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ)
steps.n: Adds هِمْ to show the lord belongs to or is associated with them
When مِن comes before this word, the ر gets a shadda (doubled) to make it stronger - this is a common rule in Arabic
This is a demonstrative pronoun used to point to people or things that are far away. In this ayah, it points back to people mentioned in previous verses.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
This is the basic form of the pronoun meaning 'they' for a group of males or mixed group
Added damma marks to show this is the subject form of 'they'. This pronoun is connected to the word الْمُفْلِحُونَ (the successful ones) in this ayah, showing who is successful.
Added مُ at the beginning to show who does the action, and ِ in the middle to complete the pattern. This changes 'succeed' into 'one who succeeds'
Added ال (the) at the beginning. This word comes after هُمُ (they are) in the ayah, showing who 'they' are
Added ُ at the end to show this describes what comes after هُمُ (they are)
Changed the ending to ون to show we're talking about multiple successful people. This is the group that هُمُ (they) refers to
Added a fatha at the end to help connect it smoothly with the next word (ٱلنَّاسِ)
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. This word connects to ٱلنَّاسِ (the people) to say 'and from the people'

steps.m: to be social, familiar with others
steps.n: This root is about human companionship and social interaction
Changes the concept of being social into a noun meaning the social beings (humans)
Changes the vowel pattern to make it a noun meaning a group
Adds emphasis by doubling the ن sound
Shows this word is being used after a preposition
Makes it refer to a specific group of people rather than just any people

This is a special word that refers to people in a general way. Here it introduces the person who 'purchases idle talk' (يَشْتَرِى لَهْوَ). Think of it like saying 'the one who' or 'anyone who' in English.
This root is about commercial transactions
Basic past tense pattern added vowels (fatha) to make 'he sold/traded'
Adding ٱ and ت changes meaning from 'trade' to 'purchase for oneself'
Adding ي changes it from past to present tense
The root letters show the basic idea of being distracted by something entertaining
Added vowel marks to turn the action into a noun meaning the entertainment itself
Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object being purchased in the phrase 'whoever purchases distracting entertainment'
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Forms a noun meaning 'speech/narrative' from root meaning 'to happen'
Makes the noun definite. This word is what is being purchased (object of يَشْتَرِى - he purchases) in this ayah
Shows this word describes or belongs to لَهْوَ (entertainment/amusement) in this ayah

steps.m: to go off the right path
steps.n: Basic meaning of going away from what is correct or true
Added vowels and combined the doubled last letter with shadda
Changed to Form IV pattern which shows making someone else do the action
The shadda and fatha at the end show this is in the subjunctive mood, used after certain words

steps.m: in order to make (someone) go astray (purpose of يَشْتَرِى - he purchases)
steps.n: Added لِ at beginning to show this is the purpose or goal of the action يَشْتَرِى (he purchases) in the ayah
This is a preposition that shows moving away from something or being about something. In this ayah, it's used with سَبِيلِ (path) to show leading away from Allah's path.
This root represents the basic concept of a path or way
Adding ي and vowels changes the word to mean a specific path or way
Added kasra at the end to show this is connected to the word after it (Allah)
Added shadda on ل to show emphasis in this divine name
Added kasra at the end to show this belongs to something (the path). The alif at the start changed to a special connecting form (ٱ) because it joins with the word before it.

steps.m: to change or be different from something
steps.n: This root carries the basic idea of difference or change
The root letters get vowel marks (َ and ْ) to make a word meaning 'other than', instead of the basic meaning of changing
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'with/by' and ِ at the end because it's connected to the word علْمٍ (knowledge) that comes after it
Connected the root letters together
Added kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the noun pattern that turns an action into a concept

steps.m: any knowledge (used after بِغَيْرِ meaning 'without any')
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this is indefinite (any knowledge) and follows the word بِغَيْرِ (without)
This root is about taking or acquiring something
The ت at start shows someone is doing this action to/for themselves
The يَ shows this is happening in the present/future tense

steps.m: he takes/adopts it (referring to سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ - the path of Allah)
steps.n: The هَا refers back to 'the path of Allah' mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: and he takes/adopts it (سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ) as mockery
steps.n: The و connects this to the previous actions in the verse. The whole phrase describes someone taking Allah's path as something to mock.
This root carries the basic meaning of mocking or making fun of something
Adding the damma vowels changes this from a verb root into a noun meaning the act of mocking
Adding the double fatha makes this word the object in the sentence

steps.m: as mockery (object of يَتَّخِذَهَا - takes it)
steps.n: The hamza (ء) changed to a و to make it easier to pronounce when used as an object meaning 'as mockery'
Basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to things far away
Added hamza with kasra to connect to next part
The word أُولَائِ becomes أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ by adding كَ at the end. This makes it a complete pointing word meaning 'those over there'. It connects to لَهُمْ (for them) later in the sentence.
Added fatha to make the pronunciation clear

steps.m: for them (referring to أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ - those people)
steps.n: Added هُمْ which shows this applies to a group of people (them). This word connects to أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ (those people) in the ayah, showing what they will get.
Noun meaning 'punishment' in nominative case with indefinite marker
Added fatha on first two letters and alif to make it a noun meaning the result of punishment
Added double dammah to make it indefinite (a punishment)

steps.m: to make light of something or treat as insignificant
steps.n: This root relates to being light, easy, or of little value
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Added مُ prefix and changed middle letter to ي to make it mean 'one who humiliates'
Added shadda for emphasis
Added tanween to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a' or 'an' in English) and in the nominative case
The word إذا is a conditional particle meaning 'when'. The و (and) is added to connect it to what comes before
Connected the root letters and changed و to ا at the end because it's a weak letter. This is the basic past tense form.

steps.m: it is being recited (to عَلَيْهِ - to him)
steps.n: Changed to passive present tense, showing that something is being recited to someone rather than someone doing the reciting. The ى at the end shows it's happening to a feminine subject (like our verses/آيات).

steps.m: it is being recited (to عَلَيْهِ - to him)
steps.n: Added alif khanjariya (small alif) above the ى to show it's a long vowel. This doesn't change the meaning, just helps with pronunciation.
This is a preposition that shows something is happening on top of, over, or upon something else. It's one of the basic prepositions in Arabic.
The ى changes to يْ before adding a pronoun ending. This is just a spelling change to help connect the next part.

steps.m: upon him (when our verses are recited)
steps.n: The هِ at the end means 'him'. In this ayah, it refers to the person who turns away when Allah's verses are recited to him.

steps.m: to show, appear, or be clear

steps.n: Changes the root into a noun by adding a long 'aa' sound at the start and 'ah' at the end to mean 'a sign'
Changes the singular to plural by adding ات
Adding نا means 'our' - shows these are Allah's signs
Adds نَا to show these are our signs
Basic past tense pattern (fatha vowels) added to make 'he turned/took charge'
Added shadda to show intensity and changed final ي to ى because it's at the end
Added dagger alif above the alif maksura to show proper pronunciation
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The prefix مُسْتَ changes the meaning from just 'being great' to 'making oneself great/acting proud' - like someone choosing to be arrogant
The ًا ending shows this word is describing how the action وَلَّىٰ (he turned away) was done
This is a comparison particle that shows something is similar to something else

steps.m: as if (describing how the person acts as if he didn't hear يَسْمَعْهَا)
steps.n: Adding أَن changes the meaning from a simple comparison to a hypothetical comparison. In this ayah, it describes how the person acts as if they hadn't heard the verses being recited.
This is a basic negative word that makes verbs mean 'did not' (like changing 'he heard' to 'he did not hear'). It has no root.

The shadda (ّ) makes the 'l' sound stronger, adding emphasis to show something definitely did not happen in the past. In this ayah, it emphasizes that the person absolutely did not listen to Allah's verses.

steps.m: he hears it (referring to ءَايٰتُنَا - our verses)
steps.n: Present tense 'he hears' (يَسْمَع) + هَا at the end meaning 'it/them' - referring back to 'our verses'
This is a basic particle that adds emphasis and certainty to a statement, like saying 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English
Adding كَ at the beginning changes the meaning from certainty to comparison, making it mean 'as if' or 'as though'. The نّ keeps the emphasis, making it a stronger comparison.
Added kasra (ِ) to show this is a preposition meaning something is inside something else. In this ayah, it shows something is inside his ears.
Changed the ي to ى with ٓ (alif khanjariyah) for writing style. This doesn't change the meaning.
The word changes from a verb root to a noun meaning the organ of hearing
Added َيْ to show exactly two ears
Added هِ at the end to show these are his ears

steps.m: to be heavy or weighed down

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to be heavy' into noun meaning 'heaviness'
The tanween (ًا) shows this word is affected by كَأَنَّ (as if) in the sentence كَأَنَّ فِىٓ أُذُنَيْهِ وَقْرًا (as if in his ears is deafness)
Added vowel marks to make it mean 'he gave good news' in the past tense
Adding shadda makes it mean doing the action a lot or to many people

steps.m: give him good news! (command to give news to him)
steps.n: Changed to a command form (give news!) and adds هُ (him) at the end to show who should receive the good news

steps.m: so give him good news! (sarcastic command in response to وَلَّىٰ مُسْتَكْبِرًا - he turned away arrogantly)
steps.n: The فَ here connects this command to the previous action of turning away arrogantly, creating a sarcastic threat
Forms a noun indicating intense or repeated punishment
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is done with or by means of punishment

steps.m: with a punishment (object of فَبَشِّرْهُ - give him glad tidings)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this is any punishment, not a specific one. This word is what he is being given 'glad tidings' of in فَبَشِّرْهُ (give him glad tidings).
Added ي between second and third root letters to show this describes something that causes intense pain. This word describes the punishment (عَذَابٍ) mentioned in the ayah.
Added tanween kasra to show this word is describing the punishment (عَذَابٍ) that comes right before it in the ayah
Added kasra (ِ) under the first letter to show how it's pronounced
Added shadda (ّ) to make the word stronger. This shows we're really emphasizing the next part about those who believe.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things, like saying 'who' or 'which' in English.
Added ال with shadda to make it more specific, like changing 'who' to 'the one who'
Added ين at the end to make it plural. This word connects to ءَامَنُوا۟ (they believed) to describe who believed.
These three letters together form the basic concept of trust and safety
Adding hamza and alif at the start changes the meaning from 'feeling safe' to 'making someone believe/trust'. Like turning 'safe' into 'made safe'
Adding وا at the end makes this about a group doing the action. This word describes what الَّذِينَ (those who) did.
Starts with و which means 'and'
Adding vowels makes it a past tense verb meaning 'he did'
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action

steps.m: and they did (connected to ءَامَنُوا - they believed)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this action with the previous one (believing). This shows these people both believed AND did good deeds.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changes verb 'he was good' into 'someone who is good' by adding alif after first letter
Adding ٱل makes it specific. The ص gets doubled (shadda) because it's a special 'sun letter' that combines with the ل
Added ات to change from 'righteous one' to 'righteous/good deeds' (makes it feminine plural)
Added ِ (kasra) at the end because it shows these good deeds are being acted upon by the verb عَمِلُوا۟ (they did)
Added fatha to show the basic form of the preposition
Added هُمْ (them) to show who the preposition points to. In this ayah, it shows that the Gardens of Paradise (جَنَّاتُ) belong to or are for them.
Added shadda to show emphasis of covering and added fatha to make it a noun. A garden is called this because it's covered with plants.
Added ة to specify one garden
Changed to feminine plural form to show multiple gardens
Added damma to show this word owns or possesses what comes after it (النَّعِيمِ - bliss/delight)

steps.m: to be in comfort and pleasure
steps.n: These letters together represent the basic idea of comfort and pleasure
Basic past tense verb made by adding the vowels 'a' and 'i' between the root letters
Added ي to make it into a noun meaning the state of comfort itself
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific and doubled the ن with shadda
Added kasra at the end to show possession and changed alif to wasl form
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed to active participle form to describe someone/something that remains or lasts
Added long alif to emphasize the eternal/permanent nature of the lasting

steps.m: ones who are remaining forever (describes the believers in Paradise)
steps.n: Added ين to make it plural, showing it describes multiple people. The َ (fatha) at the end shows this describes their state in Paradise.
Added the kasra vowel mark to show the correct pronunciation
Added ها at the end to show what is being referred to. The ها refers back to Paradise (الْجَنَّة) which is feminine in Arabic.

steps.m: to promise, to make a commitment

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun showing the actual promise itself
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence. It's connected to the word حَقًّا (truly) that comes after it.
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the basic word for 'deity'

This is the unique name for the One True God in Islam. The ال at the beginning and the shadda on ل make it specific and definite. The kasra at the end shows it belongs to or connects with something else in the sentence.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
The middle letter is doubled to make the meaning stronger
Adding tanween alif makes it describe how something is done. Here it describes how Allah's promise is true.
This is the basic pronoun meaning 'he'. It's one of the fundamental pronouns in Arabic.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what comes after it. This word connects 'he' to the descriptions that follow (ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ - the Mighty, the Wise).
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
This pattern shows someone or something that possesses this quality in an intense way
Adding ال makes it definite (like 'the' in English) and ُ shows this is one of Allah's names being used as a subject
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Forms an adjective meaning 'one who has perfect wisdom' - this is describing Allah in this ayah
Adds ال to make it definite and ُ to show this is one of Allah's names mentioned after العَزِيزُ (The Mighty)

steps.m: he created (subject of the sentence, followed by السَّمٰوٰتِ - the heavens)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fathas to show this happened in the past. This word starts the sentence and tells us what Allah did - He created the heavens.

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb meaning 'to rise' into a noun meaning 'sky' by adding اء at the end - this is a common pattern for making nouns from verbs
Added the plural ending ات with و before it because the word ends in اء
Added ٱل at the beginning to make it specific

steps.m: the heavens (object of خَلَقَ - He created)
steps.n: Added ِ at the end to show this is the object being created, and changed the long a to ٰ which is just a different way of writing it
Basic noun form showing something that is different or other than something else
Added بِ at beginning to mean 'with/by' and ِ at end to connect it to the next word عَمَدٍ (pillars), together meaning 'without pillars'
Connected the root letters and added fathas to create a plural noun for concrete objects that support things
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this word is being described by غَيْرِ (without) in the phrase 'without pillars'
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Changes from 'he saw' in the past to 'you see' in the present, like changing from past to now and from he to you
Changes from 'you see' (singular) to 'you all see' (plural) by adding و at the end
Adds نَ as a plural marker suffix that completes the masculine plural conjugation

steps.m: you all see it (referring to the heavens/السَّمٰوٰتِ)
steps.n: The هَا refers back to السَّمٰوٰتِ (the heavens) mentioned earlier in the verse
Connected the root letters with vowels to form basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the start makes the throwing action more forceful and direct
Adds conjunction prefix
Added alif khanjariya to indicate the divine subject (Allah)
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. It connects with the word ٱلْأَرْضِ (the earth) to show where something is located.
The ي changes to ى at the end of words in Arabic writing. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make a basic noun
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it comes after the word فِى (in/on)

steps.m: to be firmly fixed or anchored

steps.n: These are completely different words - رَسُول means messenger while رَوَاسِي means mountains. They are not related or transformed from one another. رَوَاسِي is the plural of رَاسِيَة meaning something firmly fixed.

steps.m: mountains (that Allah placed in the earth)
steps.n: Changed spelling to show long a with alif above (ٰ) and ى instead of ي. This is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: that/so that (connects to تَمِيدَ - it would sway)
steps.n: This is a basic particle that shows purpose - it connects the mountains being placed (أَلْقَىٰ) to preventing the earth from swaying (تَمِيدَ). It's like saying 'He put mountains on the earth SO THAT it wouldn't sway with you.'

steps.m: to sway or shake back and forth
steps.n: This is the basic concept of swaying or shaking motion
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show past tense
Added تَ at beginning to show feminine subject (earth/mountains) and changed first vowel to kasra to show present tense. This describes how the mountains prevent the earth from swaying.
Added kasra to the letter ب to help with pronunciation

steps.m: with/by you all (referring to those who see the mountains in تَرَوْنَهَا)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being addressed. This refers back to the same group mentioned in تَرَوْنَهَا (that you see them).

steps.m: to spread out or scatter widely
he spread out/scattered widely (subject of أَلْقَىٰ)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha and added shadda to show intensive spreading. This word continues the actions that Allah did, following أَلْقَىٰ (he cast/placed).

Added هِ to show this refers back to the sea (البَحْرُ) mentioned earlier in the verse
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic number word
Added ة at the end to make the number feminine
The ُ shows this word is the subject. Here it's describing how many pens there would be (if all the trees were pens).

steps.m: to be wide and deep (like a body of water)
steps.n: The noun form comes from the idea of water splitting or cutting through the land
Added vowel marks to make the basic noun form meaning a large body of water
Changed to broken plural form by adding أَ at start and ُ before last letter. This is one way Arabic makes plurals for words with 3-4 letters.
Added tanwin kasra at the end because this word is being counted by the number seven (سَبْعَةُ) in the ayah
This is the basic form of the word that connects parts of sentences, like saying 'what' or 'that which' in English. Here it connects to the verb نَفِدَتْ (ran out/depleted).
Adding the shadda (ّ) makes the 'm' sound stronger, which adds emphasis to the word, like saying 'whatever' instead of just 'what'
Basic past tense form showing something was used up completely

steps.m: it (feminine) ran out (referring to كَلِمٰتُ - words)
steps.n: Added تْ to show this is about something feminine (the words of Allah). The ْ (sukun) on ت shows this is the end of the word.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Changed from the action of speaking to one instance of speech (a word)
Changed from singular to plural by adding ات
The alif changes to an elongated alif (alif maksura) in this form

steps.m: words (subject of the sentence, belonging to اللَّهِ)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word is the main subject. In this ayah, these are specifically Allah's words that are being discussed.
Added emphasis by doubling the ل letter
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection, and changed initial alif to wasl form

steps.m: indeed/certainly (emphasizes that اللَّهَ is عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ)
steps.n: This is an emphatic particle that makes what follows more certain. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah is Mighty and Wise. It also puts the word after it (اللَّهَ) in a special case that ends with َ.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Added a small alif above to make the 'aa' sound longer
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'

steps.m: Allah (as the object being described in this ayah)
steps.n: The word for 'god' (ilah) becomes the proper name 'Allah', with a fatha mark at the end showing it connects to the words 'Mighty, Wise' that describe Allah
Adding the vowels shows this is a past tense verb about becoming mighty
Adding ي in the middle and changing the vowels makes this an intensive adjective - showing something has a lot of this quality, like saying 'very mighty'
The double damma (ٌ) at the end shows this word is being used as a standalone description, like saying 'He is mighty'

steps.m: to make decisions or judgments wisely
one who is full of wisdom
steps.n: Adding ي between the last two root letters with the vowel pattern creates a word for someone who deeply possesses this quality
The tanween (double damma) shows this is describing Allah in a general, absolute way. This word appears at the end of the ayah paired with عَزِيزٌ (All-Mighty) as divine attributes.
This is the basic negative particle that makes statements negative, like adding 'not' in English. In this ayah, it negates both 'your creation' (خَلْقُكُمْ) and 'your resurrection' (بَعْثُكُمْ).

steps.m: not at all (negates خَلْقُكُمْ and بَعْثُكُمْ)
steps.n: The shadda (ّ) adds emphasis to the negation, making it stronger - like saying 'not at all' instead of just 'not'.
Changed from basic root meaning 'create' into a noun that means 'the act of creating'. Works together with بَعْثُكُمْ (your resurrection) in this ayah.
Added كُمْ which means 'your' when talking to many people. This shows who the creation belongs to.
Added fatha to show clear pronunciation
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this negative statement with the previous one. This connects 'not your creation' with 'not your resurrection'.

steps.m: to send forth, to raise up

steps.n: Changes from action to noun meaning 'the act of raising up/resurrection'
Adds ُكُمْ to show the resurrection belongs to multiple people being addressed (you all)
This is the basic form of the exception particle. It's not derived from a root since it's a fundamental particle in Arabic.
The shadda (ّ) emphasizes the 'l' sound, making the exception stronger and clearer
The alif (ا) at the end helps connect this word smoothly to what comes after it in the sentence
Adding vowels to make a basic noun meaning 'soul' or 'self'
Added كَ at the beginning to show comparison, and ٍ at the end to show it's indefinite (like saying 'a' in English). This word is being compared to واحدة (one/single) in the ayah.
Changes from basic root to active participle form, meaning 'one'
Adds ة (taa marboota) at the end to make it feminine since it's describing نَفْسٍ (nafs) which is feminine
Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) to show this word is owned by or connected to something else. The alif changed to a long alif (ٰ) which is just a writing variation.
Basic conditional particle that shows something depends on something else
Adding shadda changes this from a conditional word to one that emphasizes what comes after it. Here it emphasizes that Allah is All-Hearing and All-Seeing.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns

This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ه at the end makes it a proper name. The أ changed to ٱ at the beginning.
Adding vowels makes this a past tense verb meaning 'to hear'
Changed from a past tense verb (he heard) to a special form that means someone who hears a lot, by adding ي
Added tanween to show this is describing Allah's perfect attribute of hearing everything

steps.m: one who sees/perceives extensively (attribute of اللَّهَ)
steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters to show this is a strong, permanent quality. When used for Allah, it means He sees everything completely and perfectly.
The tanween (ٌ) here shows this is a complete, perfect quality. For Allah's names, we capitalize to show they are perfect divine attributes.
This particle turns a statement into a question, like adding a question mark in English. Here it's asking about the verb تَرَ (you see)
Adding ل adds emphasis to show we expect the answer 'yes'
Adding م makes the question negative, like asking 'haven't you?' instead of 'have you?'
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make it past tense
Added تَ at the start to mean 'you' and simplified the end of the word to ى since it's easier to say

steps.m: you see (in أَلَمْ تَرَ - 'Do you not see?')
steps.n: Removed the ى at the end because it comes after أَلَمْ which affects the end of the word. This makes it a question 'Do you not see?'
This is a particle that emphasizes what comes after it. The shadda is an integral part of its structure.
Adding the shadda (double letter mark) and fatha marks makes this particle stronger. It connects أَلَمْ تَرَ (do you not see) to what comes after it (اللَّهَ - Allah), showing what is being seen.
Changed the root into a noun for 'one who is worshipped'

steps.m: Allah (the one true deity being acted upon)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific, doubled the ل with shadda, and added fatha at the end to show it's being acted upon by the verb يُولِجُ (he causes to enter)
Added basic vowels to connect the letters together

steps.m: he causes to enter (Allah is making something enter into something else)
steps.n: The word changed from 'entered' to 'makes enter'. The يُ at the start shows it's happening now. The middle vowel ِ makes it mean 'cause something to enter' instead of just 'enter'.
Connected the root letters with fatha-sukun pattern to form the basic noun
Added 'the' (ال) which combines with the first letter, making it doubled (with shadda)

steps.m: the night (object of يُولِجُ - merges into)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because this word is the object being acted upon by the verb يُولِجُ (merges into)
This is a preposition showing something entering or being contained within something else. In this ayah, it shows how the day merges into the night.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic word
Added alif to make a noun meaning the time when light flows
Added definite article ال and shadda on ن because it comes after ال
Added kasra at the end because it follows the word في (into) in the ayah

steps.m: to enter or make something enter into something else

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added يُ at start to show present tense and changed vowels to show causative meaning (making something happen)

steps.m: and he causes to enter (connected to يُولِجُ in previous phrase)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this to the previous phrase about causing night to enter into day
Connected the root letters with vowels to make it pronounceable
Added alif to create noun meaning 'daytime' - the time when light flows/shines forth
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ن because it comes after ال

steps.m: the daytime (object of يُولِجُ - merges/causes to enter)
steps.n: Added fatha at end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يُولِجُ (merges/causes to enter)
Basic preposition showing something is inside or within something else. Here it shows that the night enters into the day.
The ي at the end changes to ى when written at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun
Added ٱل with shadda to make it specific. The shadda appears because the ل of ٱل combines with the first ل of the word.
The kasra shows this word comes after the preposition فِي (in) in the phrase فِى ٱلَّيْلِ (in the night)

steps.m: to make something serve a purpose, to harness

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels and added shadda to show intensity and control over something

steps.m: and he made (something) serve (a purpose) (controls الشَّمْسَ وَٱلْقَمَرَ - the sun and moon)
steps.n: Added 'and' at start to connect with previous statement. This word shows Allah's control over the sun and moon mentioned right after it.
Root letters ش-م-س became the noun 'sun' by adding the vowels fatha-sukun-nothing
Added definite article ٱلْ which causes the ش to get a shadda
The َ (fatḥa) at the end shows this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Added ال to make it specific - referring to THE moon (Earth's moon)
Added و at the beginning to connect it to the previous phrase about the sun
The fatha shows it's being acted upon by the verb سَخَّرَ (subjected/made subservient)
Added damma and shadda to create a word meaning 'all'. This word is the subject of يَجْرِى (runs/flows) in this ayah, referring to both the sun and moon.

steps.m: all/every/each (as the doer, subject of يَجْرِى)
steps.n: The double-damma ending shows this word is doing the action in يَجْرِى (runs/flows), referring to how both the sun and moon flow to their appointed terms.
The word stays exactly the same - no transformation happens here

steps.m: to flow or run (like water or time)

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This word describes what each (كُلٌّ) of the sun and moon does.
Added elongation mark (ٓ) after ى for proper Quranic spelling
Basic preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to أَجَلٍ (appointed term) to show movement towards a specific time.
Added alif maksura (ى with small alif above) at the end, which is a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning
Added a small vertical line (مدّة) above the alif maksura to indicate a slight elongation in pronunciation
Added fatha marks to connect the letters and make it a noun referring to the time limit itself rather than the action of setting one

steps.m: a set term (running to it يَجْرِىٓ إِلَىٰٓ)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra to show this is the destination of يَجْرِىٓ (runs/moves towards). Each celestial body runs to its appointed term.
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show strong action
Added مُ at the start to show this describes something receiving the action
Makes it indefinite - means 'a' specified thing rather than 'the' specified thing
This is a particle that emphasizes what comes after it, like saying 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English

steps.m: and that (connects to previous statement about Allah controlling day and night)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this statement to the previous one about Allah's control over day and night
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters

Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added ت at beginning to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense
Added ون to show this is addressing multiple people
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Forms an intensive adjective describing one who has deep, inherent knowledge
Adding the two dots (tanween) at the end makes it mean 'an aware one' instead of 'the aware one'

The alif is written with a special mark (dagger alif) above the ذ

Adding كَ at the end makes it specifically for pointing to something far away

Adding بِ at the beginning changes 'that' to 'because'. This word connects to هُوَ ٱلْحَقُّ (He is the Truth) in the ayah, explaining why something is true.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'

steps.m: Allah (as the object of أَنَّ - that)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of أَنَّ (that) in this ayah

steps.m: and that/and indeed (connects to previous statement about Allah being the Truth)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this statement with the previous one. This links two important facts about Allah: that He is the Truth, and that He is the Most High.

Connected the root letters with fatha. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.

Added damma (ُ) on first letter to create a word meaning 'besides' or 'other than'
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because it comes after the word مِن (from/of)
Added هِ at the end to show this means 'besides him'
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from verb 'it was false' to noun meaning 'falsehood'

steps.m: the falsehood (opposite of ٱلْحَقُّ/the truth in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and ُ to show it's the subject. This word contrasts with 'the truth' (ٱلْحَقُّ) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
This particle emphasizes the statement that follows it. Here it emphasizes that Allah is the Most High.

steps.m: and that/and indeed (emphasizes ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلْعَلِىُّ)
steps.n: The و connects this statement to the previous one, showing both are true: that Allah is the Truth AND that Allah is the Most High
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Changed to the unique name 'Allah' by adding ال and merging letters. This special word is only used for The One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being referred to)

This is a personal pronoun that refers back to Allah in this ayah. It connects the subject (Allah) with what is being said about Him (that He is the Truth)
Changed و to ي and added shadda to show intensity of the quality

steps.m: The Most High (one of Allah's names)
steps.n: Adding ال makes this a specific title referring to Allah, showing this is one of His divine names

steps.m: The Most Great (divine attribute of Allah)
steps.n: Adding ال (the) turns this from 'great/mighty' into 'The Most Great' - a special name for Allah

steps.m: The Most Great (divine attribute of Allah describing His supreme greatness)
steps.n: The ُ shows this is describing Allah as a divine attribute in this ayah
Added أَ at the beginning to turn this into a question that expects the answer 'yes'
Added مْ at the end to add negation. When أَلَمْ comes before a verb like تَرَ (see), it asks if something has happened in the past, expecting 'yes' as the answer
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he saw' to present tense 'you see'

steps.m: you see (in أَلَمْ تَرَ - 'do you not see?')

Adding the shadda (double letter mark) makes it stronger, showing we're emphasizing a definite fact rather than just connecting ideas. In this ayah, it introduces the fact about the ships sailing.
The ُ and ْ vowel pattern creates a noun for something related to the circular/rotating meaning - in this case a ship, which has a rounded hull that cuts through water

steps.m: it/she flows/sails (referring to الْفُلْكَ, the ship)
steps.n: Added feminine present tense prefix تَ because الْفُلْكَ (ship) is feminine in Arabic. This shows the ship is doing the action now.

steps.m: it/she flows/sails (referring to الْفُلْكَ, the ship)
steps.n: Changed the final ي to ى which is just a writing style variation - doesn't change meaning
This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. In this ayah, it connects 'signs' (ءَايٰتٍ) with 'that' (ذٰلِكَ), showing where the signs can be found.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it's at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun

steps.m: the sea (location where ٱلْفُلْكَ تَجْرِى/the ships sail)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite (the sea) and kasra at the end because it comes after في (in) in the ayah. This sea is where the ships mentioned earlier (ٱلْفُلْكَ) are sailing.

steps.m: to bless, favor, or give comfort

Added ِ to show this blessing belongs to Allah (اللَّهِ follows this word)
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters

Added kasra at the end to show possession, connecting it to the word نِعْمَت (favor) that comes before it. Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.
Added alif at beginning to make the meaning causative - changing from just seeing to making others see
Added يُ to make it present tense - 'he' is showing
Added لِ to show purpose - this is showing Allah's signs for a reason

steps.m: so that he shows you all (referring to Allah showing His signs)

Added shadda (ّ) on م because it comes after a word ending in a vowel, making the م sound stronger
Added sukun (ْ) on ن to show the word ends here

steps.m: to show, make clear, be apparent

Makes the word plural by adding ات at the end and changing the middle vowel
Adds kasra at the end to show the word is being used as an object of a preposition
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) but means the same thing - signs/miracles belonging to something
Adds هِ with extension marks to show these are Allah's signs
Added kasra under إ to help with pronunciation. This is a particle that adds emphasis to statements.

steps.m: indeed, certainly (introduces a statement in this ayah)

This is a preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it connects 'the ships sail' with 'the sea', showing where the sailing happens.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this' for something close by
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show this points to something far away instead of close by
Added ل with kasra as a connecting letter to help pronunciation
Added كَ at the end to show we're referring to something specific being discussed

Changed from singular (one sign) to plural (many signs)
Changed the middle alif to an alif maqsurah for easier pronunciation
Adds the preposition 'for/to' to show purpose or ownership
Makes the word indefinite and shows it's affected by the preposition
This is a base particle word that indicates totality or completeness
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Added lam at beginning to show this is 'for' every/all

steps.m: intensely grateful (like someone who shows gratitude all the time)

steps.m: to call upon, pray to, invoke

steps.m: they called upon (subject of calling upon اللَّهَ)

Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'

steps.m: Allah (as the one being called upon in دَعَوُا۟)

a way of life or system to follow

steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this part to what happened before - the waves covering them. It shows that this next action follows as a result.

steps.m: to be on land (vs. sea), to be truthful/righteous

steps.n: Doubling the ر makes the meaning stronger

steps.m: with/by our signs (object of يَجْحَدُ - denies/rejects)

Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject (doer) of the verb يَجْحَدُ (denies/rejects) in this ayah

Adds the nominative case ending (ḍammah)

Basic meaning related to birth and parenthood
